div.p: CR2 holds the faulting linear address Read with: asm mov eax, cr2
This is the address that caused the fault, NOT the error code location.
0 = Page NOT present (No valid PTE) 1 = Protection violation (Page exists but access denied)
0 = Read access (Attempted read) 1 = Write access (Attempted write)
0 = Normal 1 = Reserved bits set in page table entry
0 = Data access 1 = Instruction fetch (NX bit violation)

ul.li: U=0: Kernel mode Scenario: Kernel tried to read unmapped address
U=0: Kernel mode Scenario: Kernel tried to write to unmapped address
U=1: User mode Scenario: User process tried to read unmapped address
U=1: User mode Scenario: User tried to read supervisor-only page
U=1: User mode Scenario: User tried to write to read-only page
R=1: Reserved bits set Scenario: Corrupt PTE with reserved bits set
